chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD C.P. No.D-1622 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE For orders on office objection For hearing of M.A. 6391/2025 For hearing of main case 11.09.2025 Mr. Muhammad Nadeem Tagar, Advocate for Petitioners Respondent No.7 Mst. Nooran (mother of Petitioner No.1) present in person Mr. Muhammad Ismail Bhutto, Addl. A.G Sindh Mr. Shahid Ahmed Shaikh, Addl. P.G. along with SHO PS Women Umerkot and ASI Niaz Muhammad PS Pangrio ---- The Petitioners are present in Court along with their Counsel so also SHO PS Women Umerkot. Petitioner No.1 Ms.t Amna has stated that she has contracted marriage with petitioner No.2 out of her own volition, free from coercion or pressure but against wishes of her parents/relatives hence being annoyed her mother Mst. Nooran (Respondent No.7) has lodged subject F.I.R. on false allegation that she has been abducted by petitioner No.2, who is her husband. The SHO PS Umerkot present today, who has recorded statement of petitioner No.1, submits that Petitioner No.1 Mst. Amna in her statement has confirmed her marriage with petitioner No.2 and denied her abduction. The SHO further submits that in the light of said statement of petitioner No.1, a report before the learned Magistrate concerned would be submitted disposing of the case. Petitioners and their counsel are satisfied and seek disposal of the petition in the said terms. Accordingly, this petition is disposed of in the above terms along with listed application. However, learned Magistrate before whom if the report disposing of the case under “C” is filed shall pass an appropriate order disposing of the case as recommended, in accordance with law.

Accordingly, this petition is disposed of in the above terms along with listed application. However, learned Magistrate before whom if the report disposing of the case under “C” is filed shall pass an appropriate order disposing of the case as recommended, in accordance with law. SHO PS Women Umerkot present today is directed to provide protection to the petitioners according to law. JUDGE JUDGE Ali Haider
